steps.title: Spot the outcome verb
steps.content: Sentence 3 says Loske “reveals clues”—a verb indicating discovery or result. This outcome must be connected to the analyzing action in sentence 2, so the transition needs to show cause and effect.

steps.title: Match the relationship to the choices
steps.content:
”In doing so” creates that cause-effect link: the discovery results from the prior action.

The other options all create wrong relationships: “however” and “in comparison” suggest contrast or comparison, while “lastly” implies sequencing rather than consequence.

This matches choice D — D is correct.
